title: Amazon EC2 - Elastic Compute Cloud
description: Master Amazon EC2 - virtual servers in the cloud. Learn about instance types, pricing models, and best practices for running workloads on AWS.
objectives: Understand what EC2 is and its core use cases
Master EC2 instance types and families
Learn all pricing models and cost optimization
Launch and manage EC2 instances effectively

sections.title: What is Amazon EC2?
sections.content:
**Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud (EC2)** is AWS's web service that provides secure, resizable compute capacity in the cloud. Think of it as renting virtual servers that you can configure however you need.

**Key Benefits:**
- Launch virtual servers in minutes
- Pay only for what you use
- Complete control over your instances
- Scale up or down automatically
- Choose from 500+ instance types

**Real-World Use Cases:**
✅ Web hosting and applications
✅ Development and testing environments
✅ Big data processing
✅ Machine learning training
✅ Gaming servers
✅ Enterprise applications
✅ Containerized workloads

sections.title: EC2 Instance Types & Families
sections.content:
AWS offers **500+ instance types** across **7 main families**, each optimized for specific workloads.

**Naming Convention:**
`t3.large` = **Family** (t) + **Generation** (3) + **Size** (large)

**Instance Families:**

**1️⃣ General Purpose (T, M, A)** - Balanced compute, memory, networking
**2️⃣ Compute Optimized (C)** - High-performance processors
**3️⃣ Memory Optimized (R, X, Z)** - Large datasets in memory
**4️⃣ Storage Optimized (I, D, H)** - High disk throughput
**5️⃣ Accelerated Computing (P, G, Inf, Trn)** - GPUs and ML accelerators
**6️⃣ HPC Optimized (Hpc)** - High-performance computing
**7️⃣ Previous Generation** - Older instance types

sections.title: General Purpose Instances (T, M, Mac)
sections.content: Ideal for applications with **balanced** compute, memory, and networking needs.

categories.name: T3/T3a - Burstable Performance
categories.notes: **CPU Credits**: Earn credits when idle, use during bursts. T3 unlimited allows sustained high CPU (extra cost).

sections.title: Compute Optimized Instances (C Family)
sections.content: **High-performance processors** for compute-intensive workloads. Up to 3.5 GHz sustained all-core turbo frequency.

categories.name: C6i/C6a/C7i - Latest Generation
categories.notes: **Best for**: CPU-bound applications that benefit from high-performance processors.

sections.title: Memory Optimized Instances (R, X, Z)
sections.content: Designed for memory-intensive applications that process **large datasets in memory**.

sections.title: Storage Optimized Instances (I, D, H)
sections.content: Optimized for workloads requiring **high sequential read/write access** to large datasets.

categories.name: I4i - NVMe SSD Storage
sizes: **i4i.large** - 2 vCPU, 16 GB RAM, 468 GB NVMe SSD
**i4i.xlarge** - 4 vCPU, 32 GB RAM, 937 GB NVMe SSD
**i4i.2xlarge** - 8 vCPU, 64 GB RAM, 1875 GB NVMe SSD
**i4i.4xlarge** - 16 vCPU, 128 GB RAM, 3750 GB NVMe SSD
**i4i.8xlarge** - 32 vCPU, 256 GB RAM, 7500 GB NVMe SSD
**i4i.16xlarge** - 64 vCPU, 512 GB RAM, 15000 GB NVMe SSD
**i4i.32xlarge** - 128 vCPU, 1024 GB RAM, 30000 GB NVMe SSD

sections.title: Accelerated Computing (P, G, Inf, Trn)
sections.content: Instances with **GPUs** and **ML accelerators** for parallel processing workloads.

categories.name: P4/P5 - ML Training (NVIDIA GPUs)
categories.notes: **P5**: Latest with NVIDIA H100 GPUs, **3200 GB/s** GPU-to-GPU bandwidth

categories.name: G5/G5g - Graphics & ML Inference
sizes: **g5.xlarge** - 4 vCPU, 16 GB RAM, 1× NVIDIA A10G GPU
**g5.2xlarge** - 8 vCPU, 32 GB RAM, 1× NVIDIA A10G GPU
**g5.4xlarge** - 16 vCPU, 64 GB RAM, 1× NVIDIA A10G GPU
**g5.8xlarge** - 32 vCPU, 128 GB RAM, 1× NVIDIA A10G GPU
**g5.16xlarge** - 64 vCPU, 256 GB RAM, 1× NVIDIA A10G GPU
**g5.12xlarge** - 48 vCPU, 192 GB RAM, 4× NVIDIA A10G GPUs
**g5.48xlarge** - 192 vCPU, 768 GB RAM, 8× NVIDIA A10G GPUs

categories.name: Inf2 - AWS Inferentia (ML Inference)
categories.notes: **Up to 70% lower cost** than GPU instances for inference

categories.name: Trn1/Trn1n - AWS Trainium (ML Training)
categories.notes: **Up to 50% cost savings** vs GPU instances for training

sections.title: EC2 Pricing Models - Save Up to 90%
sections.content: Choose the right pricing model to optimize costs significantly.

categories.name: 1. On-Demand Instances
features: Pay by the second (60 sec minimum)

categories.name: 2. Reserved Instances (RI)
features: Payment: All, Partial, or No Upfront

categories.name: 4. Savings Plans
features: Compute Savings Plan: Any EC2, Lambda, Fargate
EC2 Instance Savings Plan: Any EC2 in family

categories.name: 5. Dedicated Hosts
features: Visibility into sockets, cores, host ID

sections.title: Launching EC2 Instances - Step by Step
sections.content:
**Prerequisites:**
✅ AWS Account
✅ IAM permissions for EC2
✅ Basic networking knowledge

**Launch Steps:**

**1. Choose AMI (Amazon Machine Image)**
- Amazon Linux 2023 (Free Tier)
- Ubuntu 22.04 LTS
- Windows Server 2022
- Red Hat Enterprise Linux
- Custom AMIs

**2. Choose Instance Type**
- Free Tier: t2.micro or t3.micro
- Development: t3.small/medium
- Production: m5.large or larger

**3. Configure Instance**
- Network (VPC)
- Subnet (Availability Zone)
- Auto-assign Public IP
- IAM Role (NO hardcoded credentials!)
- User Data (startup scripts)

**4. Add Storage**
- Root volume (8 GB minimum)
- EBS types: gp3 (general), io2 (high performance)
- Instance Store (temporary)

**5. Configure Security Group**
- SSH (22) from My IP
- HTTP (80) from 0.0.0.0/0
- HTTPS (443) from 0.0.0.0/0

**6. Create Key Pair**
- Download .pem file
- Keep secure (can't download again!)

**7. Launch!**

sections.title: Connecting to EC2 Instances
sections.content:
**Linux/Mac SSH:**

```bash
# Set permissions
chmod 400 my-key.pem

# Connect
ssh -i "my-key.pem" ec2-user@<public-ip>

# Amazon Linux 2: ec2-user
# Ubuntu: ubuntu
# RHEL: ec2-user or root
```

**Windows:**
- Use PuTTY (convert .pem to .ppk)
- Use PowerShell
- Use WSL (Windows Subsystem for Linux)

**EC2 Instance Connect (Browser):**
- Click "Connect" in AWS Console
- No key needed
- Works for Amazon Linux 2 and Ubuntu

**AWS Systems Manager Session Manager:**
- No SSH needed
- No open ports needed
- Full audit trail
- IAM-based access control

sections.title: Managing EC2 Instances
sections.content:
**Instance States:**

**Pending** → **Running** → **Stopping** → **Stopped**
                    ↓
             **Terminating** → **Terminated**

**Actions:**

**Start**: Boot stopped instance
- Pays for running time
- May get new public IP (use Elastic IP to prevent)

**Stop**: Shutdown instance
- Stops compute charges
- Still pay for EBS storage
- Data preserved

**Reboot**: Restart OS
- Same IPs maintained
- No data loss

**Terminate**: Delete forever
- ⚠️ Cannot be undone
- Root volume deleted (unless configured)

**Instance Metadata:**
```bash
# Access from within instance
curl http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/instance-id
curl http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/public-ipv4
curl http://169.254.169.254/latest/user-data
```

sections.title: EC2 Security Best Practices
items: **Use IAM Roles** - Never hardcode AWS credentials on instances
**Security Groups** - Implement least privilege, restrict IPs where possible
**Key Management** - Unique keys per project, store securely, rotate regularly
**Keep Updated** - Regularly patch OS and software, enable auto-updates
**EBS Encryption** - Always encrypt volumes and snapshots at rest
**VPC Configuration** - Use private subnets for internal services
**Enable CloudWatch** - Monitor performance and set alarms
**Regular Backups** - Create AMIs and EBS snapshots frequently
**Use Systems Manager** - Centralized patch management and session access
**Enable CloudTrail** - Audit all API calls for compliance
**No Root Login** - Disable root SSH access
**Multi-Factor Auth** - Require MFA for AWS Console access
**Security Scanning** - Use AWS Inspector for vulnerability assessment

sections.title: Monitoring & Optimization
sections.content:
**CloudWatch Metrics (Basic - Free):**
- CPU Utilization
- Network In/Out
- Disk Read/Write
- Status Checks
- Updated every 5 minutes

**Detailed Monitoring ($0.10/instance/month):**
- 1-minute intervals
- Better for auto-scaling
- Faster alerting

**Status Checks:**

**System Status Check** (AWS infrastructure):
- Power, network connectivity issues
- Hardware problems
- Resolution: Stop and start instance

**Instance Status Check** (Your OS):
- Failed system startup
- Network/firewall config
- Kernel issues
- Resolution: Reboot or fix configuration

**Cost Optimization Tips:**
✅ Right-size based on CloudWatch metrics
✅ Use Auto Scaling for variable loads
✅ Stop instances when not needed
✅ Use Spot for batch jobs (90% savings)
✅ Reserved Instances for base load (72% savings)
✅ Enable Cost Allocation Tags
✅ Set up billing alarms

advantages: **Elasticity** - Scale from 1 to 10,000+ instances in minutes
**500+ Instance Types** - Perfect fit for any workload
**Multiple Pricing Models** - Save up to 90% with Spot instances
**Security** - AWS-owned data centers with extensive compliance certifications
**No Upfront Investment** - Pay only for what you use
**Automatic Backups** - EBS snapshots and AMI creation
